questions.statement: A person can acquire Indian citizenship by naturalisation if the person is ordinarily a resident of India for ________ years.
Who among the given candidates is most&nbsp;likely to acquire the Citizenship of India?
options: <span style="">A person born in India after July 1, 1987&nbsp;</span><span style="">becomes Indian citizen regardless of&nbsp;</span><span style="">citizenship of his parents.</span> /
<span style="">A person of Indian origin (PIO) ordinarily&nbsp;</span><span style="">residing in India for 3 years before making an&nbsp;</span><span style="">application for registration.</span> /
<span style="">Migrants from any neighbouring country who&nbsp;</span><span style="">have faced religious persecution in that&nbsp;</span><span style="">country.</span> /

<span style="">A PIO who came to Assam before January 1,&nbsp;</span><span style="">1966, from Bangladesh and who have been&nbsp;</span><span style="">ordinarily residents in Assam since the date of&nbsp;</span><span style="">their entry into Assam.</span> /

questions.statement: <p>The Central Ministry which deals with Citizenship is&nbsp;</p>
questions.explanation: Foreigners Division of Ministry of Home Affairs deals&nbsp;with all matters relating to visa, immigration, citizenship, overseas citizenship of India, acceptance of foreign contribution and hospitality.

questions.statement: Which among the following statements regarding citizenship in India is <strong>NOT </strong>correct?
options: <span style="">Citizenship provisions came into force on 26 November 1949.</span> /
<span style="">A person who voluntarily acquires the citizenship of a foreign country ceases to be an Indian citizen.</span> /
<span style="">A citizen of India can simultaneously be the citizen of another country.</span> /
<span style="">The Parliament has the power to make provisions pertaining to acquisition and termination of citizenship.</span> /
